title: EU Reviews Crypto Regulations: Public Feedback Sought on MiCA Framework
summary: The European Commission is asking for public input on whether its crypto regulations, known as MiCA, still work as markets and global policies change. This review could lead to updates affecting how crypto is used and regulated in the EU.
body:
The European Commission has launched a consultation to assess whether the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework, introduced in 2024, remains effective. As digital asset markets and global policies evolve, the EU wants feedback on whether its regulations are still fit for purpose.

The consultation, open until July 20, 2026, seeks input from stakeholders, including crypto businesses, consumers, and industry experts. The review will consider market developments, technological advancements, and global regulatory trends to ensure MiCA keeps pace with the industry.

This matters because any changes to MiCA could impact how crypto is traded, regulated, and used across the EU. For everyday users, this could mean adjustments to how they access and use digital assets, as well as potential changes in compliance and consumer protection measures.

If you're a crypto user or business in the EU, now is the time to voice your opinion. The consultation is open to the public, and your input could shape the future of crypto regulations in Europe. Keep an eye on the outcomes, as they may influence how you interact with digital assets in the coming years.
